text:
Assistant: Verified both ways — **"app" is a single project: `/Users/pablofernandez/src/app`** (all 235 sessions share that exact cwd). It is **not** clamping different `*-app` projects together.

To be crystal clear on your worry:
- **Grouping key is the full path** (`normalize_path(resolve_project_root(cwd))`), not the basename. So `/Users/.../src/app` and `/Users/.../Work/foo/ios-app` are **separate buckets → separate wikis**, always.
- The basename is *only the picker's display label*. Generic labels like `app`, `win`, `main`, `src` *can* collide visually (two different full-path projects both showing as "app" would appear as two separate rows with the same label) — but they're never *merged*. In your case there's only one `src/app`, so it's unambiguous.
- Your examples don't even collide: `…/ios-app` shows as **"ios-app"**, `…/win-the-day-app` shows as **"win-the-day-app"** (separate row, 18 sessions). Different labels entirely.
